key_insight: Conforming finite-element SPD systems can be preconditioned in a black-box way by exploiting local Gram/energy structure to build multilevel algebraic preconditioners.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical AMG-style solver path for difficult solid-mechanics and PDE systems where classical AMG can stall or converge very slowly.
method: Show that a local Gram representation exists exactly when a local SPSD splitting exists on the chosen DoF cover.
Build interpolation from local spectral problems derived from the Gram factor G, then coarsen with A_c = G_c^T G_c using G_c = G P.
Apply the framework to conforming FEM by extracting element-local factors from assembled element energies, enabling black-box use on H^1, H(div), and H^2 problems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robustness on multiple hard test cases, including problems where classical AMG needed more than 10^5 iterations, and notes 2–5 orders of magnitude smaller errors than some competing AMG methods at the same residual tolerance.
watch_item: The approach depends on constructing suitable local Gram factors / SPSD splittings from element blocks, which may require careful implementation and factor extraction for some discretizations.
paper_title: A black-box, multilevel algebraic preconditioning framework for conforming finite elements
